key_insight: PI-MFA reconstructs continuous flow fields with B-splines while enforcing PDE and boundary-condition physics, so the output is both smooth and physically consistent.
engineering_relevance: It provides a practical way to turn discrete simulation or measurement data into differentiable fields for analysis, visualization, and downstream PDE-based workflows.
method: Build tensor-product B-spline approximations with compact support and exact analytical derivatives.
Optimize spline control points by balancing data fidelity against PDE residuals plus initial/boundary conditions.
Use the resulting continuous fields to evaluate physical consistency without storing full-resolution data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on 1D convection-diffusion, 2D coupled Burgers, and 2D incompressible Navier-Stokes problems, where PI-MFA reduced PDE residuals, improved balance-law consistency, and outperformed standard/regularized MFA while being competitive with physics-informed neural networks.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the chosen PDE constraints and the spline resolution, so misspecified physics or insufficient basis capacity could limit reconstruction accuracy.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed B-Spline Framework for Continuous Approximation of Flow Data
